chief_complaint: A 59-year-old male presented with acute-onset dyspnea and chest pain.
history: The patient had laryngeal cancer and completed three cycles of neoadjuvant paclitaxel-cisplatin chemotherapy. He underwent a total laryngectomy and presented with symptoms that rapidly deteriorated into cardiogenic shock.
exam: Severe hypotension, third-degree atrioventricular block, and profound cardiogenic shock.
labs: Echocardiography: severely depressed left ventricular ejection fraction (< 30%) with global hypokinesis. Serum high-sensitivity troponin I levels: > 27 ng/mL.
differentials: Cardiogenic shock due to obstructive coronary artery disease
Acute kidney injury due to rhabdomyolysis
correct_ranking: Cardiogenic shock due to obstructive coronary artery disease
Acute kidney injury due to rhabdomyolysis
